sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Mallorca are in poor form, winless in their last 6 matches with 2 draws and 4 losses, struggling offensively and defensively. Deportivo Alavés show mixed results with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss in their last 5 matches, displaying slightly better consistency.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, results were tight with one draw (1-1) and one narrow Alavés win (1-0). Matches tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 9 of the last 10 head-to-head games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Mallorca rank 20th with only 2 points and a weak attack (5 goals scored), while their defense has conceded 11. Alavés, positioned 10th with 8 points, have a balanced record (6 scored, 6 conceded), indicating a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Mallorca’s inability to keep clean sheets (none this season) and tendency to concede first (5/6 matches) could be exploited by Alavés, who have shown resilience in recent games. Yellow cards are likely given Alavés’ streak of over 4.5 cards in recent matches and the referee’s average of 5.2 per game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Mallorca leads Deportivo Alavés by a score of 1-0, thanks to a well-taken goal by Takuma Asano in the 37th minute. Mallorca has been the more clinical side, capitalizing on their opportunities despite having less possession. Deportivo Alavés, on the other hand, dominated the ball but failed to convert their entries into the final third into tangible threats. The home side's defense has held firm, while the away team struggles to create clear-cut chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca has shown efficiency in attack, creating a big chance and converting it into a goal. Their defensive structure has been solid, with multiple players contributing to clearances and interceptions. Deportivo Alavés, despite controlling more of the ball and having numerous entries into the final third, lacked the cutting edge in front of goal. Their inability to register a shot on target highlights a disconnect in the final stages of their attacks. Mallorca’s duel-winning ability has also given them an edge in physical battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at the break
halftimeSections.text: For Mallorca, Takuma Asano has been the standout performer, notching the crucial goal that separates the two sides. Johan Mojica has also impressed with his contributions on both ends of the pitch, while Marc Domenech played a pivotal role in creating opportunities. On the Alavés side, players like Vicente Carlos have shown tenacity in defense, but the lack of impact from attacking players like Lucas Boyé and Abderrahman Rebbach has been evident. They will need more from their forwards to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half saw frequent shifts between the two teams. Initially, there was a back-and-forth battle, with both sides having spells of dominance in the early minutes. Mallorca started to assert themselves with a sustained attacking presence around the midway point of the half, culminating in their goal. After taking the lead, they continued to press forward with intent. Alavés responded with bursts of pressure, particularly towards the end of the half, but couldn’t translate their attacking spells into meaningful opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas to watch in the second half
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca will likely look to maintain their compact defensive shape while seeking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities. Their ability to win duels could frustrate Alavés further. For Deportivo Alavés, the focus will be on improving their finishing and making better use of their possession in dangerous areas. They need to turn their final third entries into shots on target if they hope to equalize. Disciplinary issues could also play a role, as Alavés has picked up more yellow cards, which might impact their approach.
